title: Battle Between Lord Shiva and Andhak

analysis.core_story_summary: Andhak had become so arrogant that once, he even dared to dishonour his own mother-Parvati, who at that time was living at the Mandarachal mountain. Shiva became very furious with him. Andhak attacked Shiva with his huge army.

audience_notes.children_8_12: Use simple language, explain divine names as characters in a sacred legend.
audience_notes.teens_13_17: Connect events to decision-making, identity, and consequences.
audience_notes.young_adults_18_30: Highlight ethics, leadership, and social relevance.
audience_notes.devotional_traditional: Preserve sacred framing and theological terms respectfully.
audience_notes.global_general: Keep cultural context brief and avoid insider assumptions.

sensitivity_notes: Contains conflict imagery that may need age-appropriate framing.
Includes intense mythic elements (curses, demons, destruction).

redesigned_story_by_demographic.children_8_12: **Battle Between Lord Shiva and Andhak — Kids (8-12):** Andhak had become so arrogant that once, he even dared to dishonour his own mother-Parvati, who at that time was living at the Mandarachal mountain. Shiva became very furious with him. Andhak attacked Shiva with his huge army. This story teaches respect for nature. Think of it like a legend that helps us choose kindness, courage, and respect.

Shiva became very furious with him. Andhak attacked Shiva with his huge army. For teens, the key idea is respect for nature. It shows how choices, discipline, and character shape outcomes.
